title: Upstream data catalog manifest
$comment: A 'catalog' is an upstream data resource — an archive, survey release, compilation, or bibliographic service — that this repository draws from. The term 'catalog' is used rather than 'source' to avoid collision with the standard astronomy meaning of 'source' as an astronomical object. Object-identity schemas will be introduced in Phase 1 and will use 'object' or 'transient', not 'source'.

id.description: Stable lowercase identifier matching the manifest filename stem.

items.$comment: This enum is the authoritative domain vocabulary. Add values here when introducing a new astrophysical domain — do not introduce new values in individual manifests.

domain.description: Astrophysical domain tags. Values must come from the enum above. Selection qualifiers such as 'low-redshift', 'volume-limited', and 'calibration' are not domain tags — record them in the manifest role or in selection cuts on validation sets.

role.description: One-sentence description of this catalog's role in the evidence registry.

release.description: Data release identifier as used by the upstream authority (e.g. 'DR3', 'v2.0', 'SN5YR'). Required before any adapter is built for this catalog; optional at the candidate and planned stages.

provenance.$comment: Records who is authoritative for this catalog, when it was accessed, and what the license or terms are. This is not artifact provenance; generated artifacts use artifact-provenance sidecars.
